title: Silky Smoky Ukrainian Chicken Soup
description: An earthy and silky monsoon dish featuring diabetic-friendly-friendly chicken stock infused with smoky flavors, perfect for a comforting dinner.

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Mashed lightly with the back of a knife

ingredients.name: Chicken Stock
ingredients.notes: Thickened with cornstarch and water to achieve a silky texture

ingredients.name: Bay leaves
ingredients.notes: Freshly chopped for extra depth of flavor

instructions: Place chicken bones in a large pot, cover with water, and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, let simmer for about an hour.
In another pan, sauté chopped onions until translucent, then add mashed garlic. Cook briefly and set aside.
Drain the broth from the pot of chicken bones, reserve any excess for thickening later. Add onion-garlic mixture to the reserved broth.
Add carrots, potatoes, celery, bay leaves, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, salt, pepper, and remaining water. Season with a pinch of sugar for sweetness balance if needed.
Simmer all ingredients together until vegetables are tender, about 20-30 minutes.
Blend the soup until smooth in batches using an immersion blender or regular blender, adding back any seasoning as it blends. If necessary, return to simmering and blend again.
Thicken the blended soup by mixing cornstarch with a small amount of hot water, then stir into the pot and let cook for another minute to thicken slightly.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
